jokes.text: Hey, Louie, Louie, Louie! What? Aren't you forgetting something? I've never seen you go up and visit the boss without bringing some token of your esteem.
jokes.setup: Louie is rushing to see his boss after a frantic phone call
jokes.punchline: Driver points out Louie's sycophantic gift-giving pattern
jokes.explanation: Good character observation about Louie's brown-nosing behavior, sets up the running gag of inappropriate gifts

jokes.text: Jeff, uh, give me the golf balls. You already gave him the golf balls, Lou.
jokes.setup: Louie desperately needs a gift for his boss
jokes.punchline: He's already exhausted his go-to gift option
jokes.explanation: Efficient setup showing Louie's repetitive gift-giving and current desperation

jokes.text: You got the cigars, that cheap wine, a stuffed owl and a Waterpik.
jokes.setup: Inventory of lost and found items to choose from
jokes.punchline: Bizarre collection ending with oddly specific Waterpik
jokes.explanation: Good absurd escalation in the list, stuffed owl is particularly random

jokes.text: He's going to give God a Waterpik?
jokes.setup: Discussion of Louie going 'upstairs' to see 'the guy upstairs'
jokes.punchline: Driver misunderstands, thinks Louie is literally going to meet God
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection and absurdist imagery - the idea of bribing God with dental equipment is genuinely funny

jokes.text: I'll explain it to him later.
jokes.setup: The absurd God/Waterpik misunderstanding
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal as if it's a minor theological clarification
jokes.explanation: Nice deadpan follow-up that extends the God confusion joke

jokes.text: How about you sit here?
jokes.setup: Louie asking Jeff to make himself comfortable
jokes.punchline: Awkward repositioning suggesting Louie is stalling/nervous
jokes.explanation: Minor physical comedy beat showing Louie's discomfort before delivering bad news

jokes.text: You mean you kept all that money? Yeah. Oh... now I know what Ratledge is so hot about.
jokes.setup: Jeff learning Louie has been embezzling
jokes.punchline: Jeff's slow, understanding reaction
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jeff's innocent understanding of the situation

jokes.text: Boy, you got lucky!
jokes.setup: Learning that Ratledge doesn't know Louie is the thief
jokes.punchline: Jeff's relief for Louie, not realizing what's coming
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic irony - Jeff's relief just before learning he's the scapegoat

jokes.text: But you didn't.
jokes.setup: Jeff's relief that Louie got lucky
jokes.punchline: Louie's ominous reversal
jokes.explanation: Perfectly timed reversal with maximum economy - three words that completely flip the situation

jokes.text: Yeah... that's what makes letting you go so painful.
jokes.setup: Jeff protesting his innocence
jokes.punchline: Louie's mock sympathy while screwing over an innocent person
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark character moment - Louie's faux sympathy makes his betrayal even more callous

jokes.text: You know Ratledge... I mean, one minute he's a maniac, the next minute, he's a pussycat.
jokes.setup: Louie trying to convince Jeff to go along with the plan
jokes.punchline: Describing his boss as having extreme mood swings
jokes.explanation: Good character detail about the boss, shows Louie's manipulative reasoning

jokes.text: I think this stinks. I think you stink. I think the deal stinks. The whole thing stinks!
jokes.setup: Jeff reluctantly agreeing to Louie's plan
jokes.punchline: Escalating repetition of 'stinks' to express total disgust
jokes.explanation: Good escalating rant showing Jeff's frustration and moral clarity

jokes.text: Jeff, I'm worried you're going into this with a bad attitude.
jokes.setup: Jeff's explosive rant about how everything stinks
jokes.punchline: Louie's massive understatement of Jeff's justified anger
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan understatement - Louie's obliviousness to the magnitude of his betrayal is perfectly in character

jokes.text: I don't want your dirty money. Great! I wouldn't respect you if you took it.
jokes.setup: Louie offering Jeff money as compensation
jokes.punchline: Louie's twisted logic praising Jeff's refusal
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Louie's ability to twist any situation to make himself look good

jokes.text: You bit the hand that fed you. Now, if it was up to me, you would be going straight to jail.
jokes.setup: Louie fake-firing Jeff for Ratledge's benefit
jokes.punchline: Louie pretending moral outrage about Jeff biting the hand that feeds him
jokes.explanation: Darkly ironic - Louie using the exact phrase that describes his own behavior while condemning Jeff

jokes.text: In a few days... we'll all be missing old Jeff. Not me. Me, either. Oh, no.
jokes.setup: Louie trying to seem sentimental about Jeff
jokes.punchline: Ratledge's immediate dismissal and Louie's quick agreement
jokes.explanation: Good beat showing Louie's quick reversal when his boss disagrees

jokes.text: Why, if you had seen me three years ago, you would have said... Who is this bum? Exactly!
jokes.setup: Jeff thanking Ratledge for giving him a job that changed his life
jokes.punchline: Ratledge immediately agreeing Jeff used to be a bum
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - Jeff expecting a polite response but Ratledge bluntly agrees

jokes.text: Jeff was special. He was like a son to me; a beautiful, black son.
jokes.setup: Louie expressing fake sentiment about Jeff
jokes.punchline: Awkward racial qualifier that makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Cringeworthy character moment showing Louie's tone-deaf attempt at sentiment

jokes.text: But I think what he is trying to say is that Jeff is not a thief. Am I right?
jokes.setup: Multiple people insisting Jeff is not a thief
jokes.punchline: Driver helpfully clarifying the obvious repeated point
jokes.explanation: Mild joke based on over-explaining an obvious repeated sentiment

jokes.text: You... you speak English? Yeah. You've just been promoted. Get in the cage.
jokes.setup: Louie needing to replace Jeff immediately
jokes.punchline: Absurdly low qualification standard for the job
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurd moment showing both Louie's desperation and his low standards

jokes.text: I told them I was your attorney.
jokes.setup: Jeff is surprised to see Louie at jail
jokes.punchline: Louie's ridiculous claim to be Jeff's lawyer
jokes.explanation: Absurd image of Louie as anyone's attorney, especially his victim's

jokes.text: Look, prison is not such a bad place. I mean, they give you a bed. They give you food. They give you a toilet. That's pretty much life as we know it, Jeff.
jokes.setup: Louie trying to convince Jeff that prison wouldn't be so bad
jokes.punchline: Reducing life to its most basic elements to make prison sound appealing
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny minimalist view of life, perfectly showing Louie's desperation and callousness

jokes.text: You'd have a much easier time in the joint than I would... A tasty morsel like me wouldn't last five minutes.
jokes.setup: Louie explaining why Jeff should go to prison instead of him
jokes.punchline: Describing himself as a 'tasty morsel' vulnerable to prison violence
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor - 'tasty morsel' is a perfect phrase for Louie's self-aware vulnerability

jokes.text: Jeff, what if I made a nice donation to the United Negro College Fund?
jokes.setup: Louie desperately trying to negotiate with Jeff
jokes.punchline: Offering a charitable donation as if it's equivalent to going to prison
jokes.explanation: Shows Louie's tone-deaf desperation and complete lack of proportion about the situation

jokes.text: It's just that, uh, I was kicking myself the other day because I never gave you anything for the lovely Mrs. Ratledge.
jokes.setup: Louie needs to butter up his boss
jokes.punchline: His obviously desperate attempt to curry favor
jokes.explanation: Standard brown-nosing comedy consistent with Louie's character

jokes.text: It's a Norelco Ladybug shaver.
jokes.setup: Louie giving Ratledge a gift for his wife
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and inappropriate gift choice
jokes.explanation: Absurd gift choice that shows Louie's desperation and poor judgment in gift-giving

jokes.text: Because he likes me.
jokes.setup: Ratledge asking why Jeff didn't speak up if he's innocent
jokes.punchline: Louie's simple, almost childlike explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and simplicity - reveals both the friendship and Louie's emotional immaturity

jokes.text: You're trying to take the rap for another man, huh? Well, it won't work. You think I'd go easier on you... Well, you're right, I would.
jokes.setup: Ratledge seeming to reject Louie's noble gesture
jokes.punchline: Immediate admission that Louie is right about getting easier treatment
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - sets up noble rejection then immediately undercuts it with honesty

jokes.text: After all... I'm the thief. Enough already. Get out of here.
jokes.setup: Louie continuing to insist he's the real thief
jokes.punchline: Ratledge's exasperated dismissal of what he thinks is false nobility
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing the irony that Louie can't convince anyone of the truth

jokes.text: If you liked my confession, you're gonna love this.
jokes.setup: Louie calling Alex up to verify his story
jokes.punchline: Treating it like entertainment for Ratledge
jokes.explanation: Good meta-theatrical moment where Louie treats the serious situation like a performance

jokes.text: And Jeff took the rap for it because... Because he likes him.
jokes.setup: Alex confirming Louie's story about the theft
jokes.punchline: Ratledge completing the simple, childlike explanation
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the earlier explanation, now accepted as truth

jokes.text: Now you get out of here, you scamp. And you take your sidekick with you.
jokes.setup: Ratledge addressing Louie after resolving the situation
jokes.punchline: Affectionate 'scamp' showing he's not really angry
jokes.explanation: Nice character beat showing Ratledge's paternal affection despite the situation

jokes.text: Now you get out of here, you scamp. And you take your sidekick with you.
jokes.setup: Ratledge has resolved the situation
jokes.punchline: Ratledge's almost affectionate dismissal calling them scamps
jokes.explanation: Charming character moment with Ratledge's unexpected warmth

jokes.text: Why yes, I do, sir, but I cheat on my score.
jokes.setup: Ratledge asking if Louie plays golf
jokes.punchline: Louie's unnecessary honesty about cheating
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Louie's compulsive honesty about the wrong things

jokes.text: I stole... I lied... I betrayed a friend... and now I got extra money in the bank and a 9:00 tee-off time on Saturday.
jokes.setup: Louie trying to understand why crime paid off for him
jokes.punchline: Listing terrible acts followed by their pleasant rewards
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy structure - the escalating list of crimes contrasted with mundane rewards

jokes.text: I can't explain the feeling I get from being honest. I mean, it's like trying to explain, uh, April in Paris.
jokes.setup: Louie asks Alex to explain honesty
jokes.punchline: Alex's romantic, poetic comparison to Paris in April
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Alex's romantic idealism about morality

jokes.text: I mean, it's like trying to explain, uh, April in Paris. You can't understand it unless you've been there.
jokes.setup: Alex trying to explain the feeling of being honest
jokes.punchline: Using romantic Paris metaphor for moral behavior
jokes.explanation: Nice metaphor showing Alex's romantic view of moral behavior

jokes.text: Oh! I understand April in Paris. It's like having Havana cigars, a great drink of booze and being off scot-free.
jokes.setup: Alex's romantic metaphor about honesty
jokes.punchline: Louie's material interpretation focused on getting away with crime
jokes.explanation: Excellent character contrast - Louie completely misses Alex's point and reframes it in criminal terms

jokes.text: Let's face it, Reiger, crime pays.
jokes.setup: Louie's moral confusion about getting away with theft
jokes.punchline: Blunt statement contradicting basic moral assumptions
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and darkness - simple statement that subverts moral expectations

jokes.text: That's the price you have to pay. Because you're a thief, you have to go through life believing that everybody else is a thief.
jokes.setup: Louie hides his money and assumes others will steal
jokes.punchline: Alex's philosophical observation about the cost of dishonesty
jokes.explanation: Excellent philosophical comedy about the psychological cost of being dishonest

jokes.text: That's why you have to hide your stash in some crummy little air duct. Boy, you lead a real crummy little life, you know that? You're a frightened man, Louie.
jokes.setup: Alex explaining the price of being dishonest
jokes.punchline: Pointing out that Louie's criminal success makes him paranoid and miserable
jokes.explanation: Good moral observation - shows the hidden cost of Louie's 'successful' crime

jokes.text: You're a frightened man, Louie.
jokes.setup: Alex observes Louie hiding his money
jokes.punchline: Alex's psychological insight cuts through Louie's bravado
jokes.explanation: Good character insight that reveals the cost of Louie's philosophy

jokes.text: Hey, what about me, Louie? What about me, huh? Aren't you afraid I'm going to steal your stuff? I know where you're hiding it. You?! The honest man?! Nah!
jokes.setup: Alex pointing out Louie's paranoia about thieves
jokes.punchline: Louie's dismissive reaction to Alex as a threat
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Louie's trust in Alex despite his general paranoia

jokes.text: You?! The honest man?! Nah!
jokes.setup: Alex suggests Louie should worry about Alex stealing from him
jokes.punchline: Louie's dismissal based on Alex's honesty
jokes.explanation: Good irony in Louie's trust of Alex based on the very quality he mocks

jokes.text: Reiger!
jokes.setup: Louie realizing his money might be gone
jokes.punchline: Panicked realization that he trusted the wrong person
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending beat - single word that implies Louie's worst fear about trusting someone
